brightsmith_agentAttribution.reasoning: MCP zone (Zone 5) registration of get_regional_price_parity tool on FutureProofMCPServer. Thin read-only passthrough over consumable.regional_price_parities (Gold). No derivation, no new data — the tool normalizes a user-supplied state identifier (USPS abbr / full name / FIPS) to a FIPS code, performs a point query via query_iceberg_simple with filters={'state_fips': fips}, and reshapes the resulting row into a Gemma-facing payload. Of the 15 Gold columns, 13 are read (record_id and promoted_at are excluded as pipeline metadata). Of those 13, 8 are DIRECT passthroughs to same-named response fields, 4 are BUNDLED into the adjusted_examples struct with shortened keys ('30k','50k','75k','100k'), and 1 (verification_status) is RENAMED to data_source for Gemma-facing clarity per Bronze staff-review Condition 7. governance.quality_tier ('partial_verification') and governance.owner ('@data-steward') are FRAMEWORK-INJECTED via attach_governance() reading the top-level contract YAML. The verified_only=true strict mode refuses estimate rows and returns a structured null response so Gemma can surface per-row provenance without joining back to Silver. No transformation of value content occurs at this layer — the only 'computation' is struct-reshaping and a field rename.

documentation.description: Register the get_regional_price_parity MCP tool on the FutureProof MCP server. The tool performs a point lookup on consumable.regional_price_parities keyed by normalized state_fips (accepts USPS abbr, full state name, or FIPS as input) and returns a Gemma-facing payload with 9 top-level fields: state_name, state_abbr, state_fips, census_region, rpp_all_items, purchasing_power_multiplier, cost_tier, adjusted_examples (struct), data_source (renamed from verification_status per Bronze Condition 7), and data_year. The four adjusted_Nk Gold columns are bundled into an adjusted_examples struct with short keys ('30k','50k','75k','100k'). No transformation at this layer — the tool is a read-only passthrough with struct-reshaping and a single rename. verified_only=true strict mode refuses estimate rows with a structured null response. Unknown-state and missing-row paths also return structured nulls. governance.{table, quality_tier, owner} are injected by attach_governance() reading the top-level contract YAML.

fields.name: record_id
fields.description: Pipeline surrogate key (prefix='rpc') — NOT read by this tool

fields.name: state_fips
fields.description: 2-digit zero-padded state FIPS; used as the lookup key and also returned in the payload

fields.name: verification_status
fields.description: Per-row provenance ('bea_official' for 8 BEA-verified states; 'estimate' for 43 others) — RENAMED to data_source in the tool response per Bronze Condition 7

fields.name: promoted_at
fields.description: Gold promote timestamp — NOT read by this tool

documentation.description: State-input normalization module used by both BEA RPP tools. Exposes normalize_state_input (accepts USPS abbr, full state name, or FIPS, case-insensitive, returns a 2-digit FIPS or None) and FIPS_TO_STATE_NAME lookup for null-case error messages. Pure function, unit-testable. Built on Silver's src/silver/_us_state_reference.py (FIPS_TO_USPS, FIPS_TO_CENSUS_REGION) extended with reverse USPS_TO_FIPS and STATE_NAME_TO_FIPS lookups per the spec's Implementation Notes.

fields.name: normalize_state_input
fields.description: (raw: Any) -> str | None — returns 2-digit FIPS or None

fields.name: FIPS_TO_STATE_NAME
fields.description: 51-entry FIPS -> canonical state name map

documentation.description: Top-level contract YAML read by FutureProofMCPServer.attach_governance to inject governance.quality_tier and governance.owner into every tool response. The contract file stores table, owner, and quality_tier at the top level of the YAML (not nested under metadata/schema), so the framework's list_contracts() cannot surface those fields — this project-local override reads the YAML directly and caches by table name. quality_tier is parsed from the folded scalar via _extract_quality_tier_token which returns the first whitespace/em-dash-separated token ('partial_verification').

fields.name: table
fields.description: 'consumable.regional_price_parities'

fields.name: owner
fields.description: '@data-steward'

fields.name: quality_tier
fields.description: 'partial_verification' (first token of the folded scalar)

data.state_name.transformationDescription: DIRECT passthrough. Same name in the response.

data.state_abbr.transformationDescription: DIRECT passthrough. Same name in the response.

data.state_fips.transformationDescription: DIRECT passthrough in the response, but ALSO the lookup key. The handler calls normalize_state_input(raw_state) to convert the user's USPS/name/FIPS input to a canonical 2-digit FIPS, then queries consumable.regional_price_parities with filters={'state_fips': fips}. The value returned to Gemma is the Gold column verbatim.

data.census_region.transformationDescription: DIRECT passthrough. Same name in the response.

data.rpp_all_items.transformationDescription: DIRECT passthrough. Same name in the response. BEA national=100 index scale preserved.

data.purchasing_power_multiplier.transformationDescription: DIRECT passthrough at full precision (pre-review Advisory #1 — the caller must be able to reconstruct arithmetic exactly).

data.cost_tier.transformationDescription: DIRECT passthrough. Same name in the response. 5-bucket enum (very_high | high | average | low | very_low) already derived at Gold from the BT-106 frozen breakpoints — MCP layer does not reclassify.

data.adjusted_examples.transformationDescription: BUNDLED (struct-reshape only, no value mutation). The four Gold adjusted_Nk columns are packed into a struct with short keys: {'30k': adjusted_30k, '50k': adjusted_50k, '75k': adjusted_75k, '100k': adjusted_100k}. Built client-side in _rpp_row_to_payload(). No rounding or recomputation — the already-rounded Gold values are copied verbatim. Short keys ('30k' vs. 'adjusted_30k') are a Gemma-facing ergonomics choice.

data.data_source.transformationDescription: RENAMED passthrough. verification_status from Gold is surfaced as data_source in the Gemma-facing response. Value content is unchanged: 'bea_official' for the 8 BEA-verified states; 'estimate' for the other 43. This is the wire-level implementation of Bronze staff-review Condition 7 (MCP half) — Gemma can hedge numeric precision when data_source='estimate'. The verified_only=true strict mode ALSO branches on this field and returns a structured null response for estimate rows.

data.data_year.transformationDescription: DIRECT passthrough. Same name in the response. Single-vintage contract (2024) propagated from raw-ingest-bea-rpp through Silver and Gold.

row_count.transformationDescription: FRAMEWORK: always 1 on success; omitted on structured-null paths.

governance.table.transformationDescription: FRAMEWORK: injected by BaseMCPServer.attach_governance — the fully-qualified table name 'consumable.regional_price_parities'.

governance.quality_tier.transformationDescription: FRAMEWORK: injected by FutureProofMCPServer.attach_governance override. Reads the top-level quality_tier from the contract YAML, runs _extract_quality_tier_token to strip the trailing folded-scalar prose, and returns the canonical token 'partial_verification'. Cached by table name.

governance.owner.transformationDescription: FRAMEWORK: injected by FutureProofMCPServer.attach_governance override. Reads the top-level owner from the contract YAML ('@data-steward'). Cached by table name.

record_id.reason: Pipeline surrogate key (prefix='rpc') not needed by MCP consumers. state_fips is the natural lookup key.

promoted_at.reason: Gold promote timestamp not needed by MCP consumers. Governance metadata is attached separately via attach_governance().

brightsmith_agentAttribution.reasoning: MCP zone (Zone 5) registration of compare_purchasing_power tool on FutureProofMCPServer. Convenience tool for the 'What if I move?' scenario — fetches two RPP rows (one per state) from consumable.regional_price_parities and computes adjusted_salary = round(salary * purchasing_power_multiplier, 2) at tool-call time for each state, plus difference and difference_pct. Unlike get_regional_price_parity this tool DOES compute at tool-call time, but only three derived scalars (adjusted_salary per side, absolute difference, and difference_pct), all using banker's rounding (Python round() == DuckDB ROUND(), per IEEE 754 round-half-to-even) to match Gold and DQ-engine semantics. The computation does not materialize any new dataset or column — it is a per-request derivation passed straight to the response. Each per-state side payload carries 6 fields: state_name, state_abbr (DIRECT), cost_tier (DIRECT), purchasing_power_multiplier (DIRECT full precision), data_source (RENAMED from verification_status per Bronze Condition 7), and adjusted_salary (DERIVED per-request). The adjusted_Nk columns from Gold are NOT read by this tool — the tool derives a fresh adjusted value from the user-supplied salary. state_fips is used internally as the lookup key but is not exposed in the compact side payloads. governance.{table, quality_tier, owner} are framework-injected via attach_governance() as in tool 1. verified_only=true strict mode refuses mixed-provenance requests where either state has verification_status='estimate'.

documentation.description: Register the compare_purchasing_power MCP tool on the FutureProof MCP server. The tool accepts (salary, state_a, state_b, verified_only) and returns a side-by-side purchasing-power comparison. For each state it normalizes input to FIPS, queries consumable.regional_price_parities with filters={'state_fips': fips}, and builds a compact per-side payload: state_name, state_abbr, adjusted_salary (derived per-request as round(salary * purchasing_power_multiplier, 2)), cost_tier, purchasing_power_multiplier (full precision), data_source (renamed from verification_status per Bronze Condition 7). Top-level the response also carries salary (echo of input), difference = round(state_b.adjusted_salary - state_a.adjusted_salary, 2), and difference_pct = round(difference / state_a.adjusted_salary * 100, 2). Null-case paths: invalid salary, unknown state, same state twice (after normalization), missing row, verified_only strict mode refusing mixed-provenance request. The four adjusted_Nk Gold columns are NOT read — this tool derives a fresh adjusted salary from the user-supplied input. governance.{table, quality_tier, owner} are injected by attach_governance().

fields.name: record_id
fields.description: Pipeline surrogate key — NOT read by this tool

fields.name: state_fips
fields.description: Used internally as the lookup key; NOT surfaced in the compact side payloads

fields.name: census_region
fields.description: Read by _fetch_rpp_row via RPP_QUERY_FIELDS but NOT surfaced in the compact side payloads

fields.name: rpp_all_items
fields.description: Read by _fetch_rpp_row but NOT surfaced in the compact side payloads

fields.name: purchasing_power_multiplier
fields.description: Surfaced in each side payload at full precision AND used as the multiplier input for adjusted_salary derivation

fields.name: adjusted_30k
fields.description: Read by _fetch_rpp_row but NOT surfaced by this tool — adjusted_salary is derived per-request from the user-supplied salary

fields.name: adjusted_50k
fields.description: Read by _fetch_rpp_row but NOT surfaced by this tool — adjusted_salary is derived per-request from the user-supplied salary

fields.name: adjusted_75k
fields.description: Read by _fetch_rpp_row but NOT surfaced by this tool — adjusted_salary is derived per-request from the user-supplied salary

fields.name: adjusted_100k
fields.description: Read by _fetch_rpp_row but NOT surfaced by this tool — adjusted_salary is derived per-request from the user-supplied salary

fields.name: verification_status
fields.description: Per-row provenance — RENAMED to data_source in each per-side payload per Bronze Condition 7. Also consumed by verified_only strict-mode branch.

fields.name: data_year
fields.description: Read by _fetch_rpp_row but NOT surfaced in the compact side payloads

fields.name: promoted_at
fields.description: Gold promote timestamp — NOT read by this tool

documentation.description: State-input normalization module (same as tool 1). Called twice per compare_purchasing_power invocation — once for state_a, once for state_b. Both inputs accept USPS abbr / full name / FIPS, case-insensitive. Returns None for unknown states; the handler surfaces a structured-null response in that case.

User-supplied salary argument to compare_purchasing_power. Validated by _validate_salary (rejects None, bool, non-numeric, NaN, inf, <=0, and >=10_000_000). Used as the multiplicand for per-state adjusted_salary derivation. Not stored anywhere — lives for the lifetime of the request. Modeled as a named input so the adjusted_salary, difference, and difference_pct lineage has a traceable non-Gold source.

fields.name: salary
fields.description: Positive, < 10_000_000, finite; validated at request time

documentation.description: Same contract YAML read by tool 1. Reused via the _contract_cache on FutureProofMCPServer so the second tool's attach_governance call is a cache hit, not a re-read.

data.salary.transformationDescription: ECHO of the validated user-supplied salary, coerced to float. Not sourced from Gold.

data.state_a.state_name.transformationDescription: DIRECT passthrough from the row fetched for state_a's normalized FIPS.

data.state_a.state_abbr.transformationDescription: DIRECT passthrough from the row fetched for state_a.

data.state_a.adjusted_salary.transformationDescription: DERIVED at tool-call time as round(salary * state_a.purchasing_power_multiplier, 2) via Python's built-in round() (IEEE 754 round-half-to-even / banker's rounding) to match DuckDB's default ROUND() and the Gold adjusted_Nk rounding mode. Not sourced from the Gold adjusted_Nk columns — the tool derives a fresh adjusted value from the user-supplied salary so arbitrary salaries (not just 30k/50k/75k/100k) are supported.

data.state_a.cost_tier.transformationDescription: DIRECT passthrough from the row fetched for state_a. 5-bucket enum already derived at Gold.

data.state_a.purchasing_power_multiplier.transformationDescription: DIRECT passthrough at full precision (pre-review Advisory #1).

data.state_a.data_source.transformationDescription: RENAMED passthrough. verification_status from Gold surfaces as data_source in each per-side payload per Bronze staff-review Condition 7 (MCP half). Value content unchanged ('bea_official' | 'estimate'). The verified_only=true strict mode also branches on this field and refuses the entire comparison when either side is an estimate.

data.state_b.state_name.transformationDescription: DIRECT passthrough from the row fetched for state_b's normalized FIPS.

data.state_b.state_abbr.transformationDescription: DIRECT passthrough from the row fetched for state_b.

data.state_b.adjusted_salary.transformationDescription: DERIVED at tool-call time as round(salary * state_b.purchasing_power_multiplier, 2) via Python's built-in round() (banker's rounding, matching DuckDB ROUND() and Gold adjusted_Nk semantics). Independent of the Gold adjusted_Nk columns.

data.state_b.cost_tier.transformationDescription: DIRECT passthrough from the row fetched for state_b.

data.state_b.data_source.transformationDescription: RENAMED passthrough per Bronze Condition 7 — same semantics as data.state_a.data_source but for the second state. Both sides carry per-row provenance to the wire.

data.difference.transformationDescription: DERIVED at tool-call time as round(state_b.adjusted_salary - state_a.adjusted_salary, 2). Implicitly depends on BOTH fetched rows' purchasing_power_multiplier AND the user-supplied salary. Banker's rounding applied to match the per-side adjusted_salary rounding mode.

data.difference_pct.transformationDescription: DERIVED at tool-call time as round(difference / state_a.adjusted_salary * 100, 2). Guarded against division-by-zero with a 0.0 fallback (pathological case — cannot occur within the valid RPP domain). Banker's rounding applied.

row_count.transformationDescription: FRAMEWORK: always 2 on success (one row per side); omitted on structured-null paths.

governance.quality_tier.transformationDescription: FRAMEWORK: injected by FutureProofMCPServer.attach_governance override. Canonical token 'partial_verification' extracted from the folded scalar in the contract YAML. Cache hit (tool 1 primed the _contract_cache in the same server instance).

governance.owner.transformationDescription: FRAMEWORK: injected by FutureProofMCPServer.attach_governance override. '@data-steward' from the contract YAML. Cache hit.

record_id.reason: Pipeline surrogate key not needed by MCP consumers. state_fips is the internal lookup key.

promoted_at.reason: Gold promote timestamp not needed by MCP consumers. Governance metadata is attached separately via attach_governance().

state_fips.reason: Used internally by _fetch_rpp_row as the lookup key, but compare_purchasing_power's compact per-side payload intentionally omits state_fips. state_name and state_abbr are sufficient for Gemma-facing display; callers who need state_fips should use get_regional_price_parity instead.

census_region.reason: Read from Gold via RPP_QUERY_FIELDS but not surfaced in compare_purchasing_power's compact per-side payload. Kept out of the response to reduce token footprint for the two-state comparison.

rpp_all_items.reason: Read from Gold via RPP_QUERY_FIELDS but not surfaced. Full-precision purchasing_power_multiplier is sufficient for the comparison; rpp_all_items would be redundant (ppm = 100 / rpp_all_items).

data_year.reason: Read from Gold via RPP_QUERY_FIELDS but not surfaced. Omitted to reduce token footprint; available via get_regional_price_parity if needed.

adjusted_30k.reason: Read from Gold via RPP_QUERY_FIELDS (shared _fetch_rpp_row) but NOT used by this tool — adjusted_salary is derived per-request from the user-supplied salary, not selected from the four frozen anchors.

adjusted_50k.reason: Read from Gold via RPP_QUERY_FIELDS (shared _fetch_rpp_row) but NOT used by this tool — adjusted_salary is derived per-request from the user-supplied salary.

adjusted_75k.reason: Read from Gold via RPP_QUERY_FIELDS (shared _fetch_rpp_row) but NOT used by this tool — adjusted_salary is derived per-request from the user-supplied salary.

adjusted_100k.reason: Read from Gold via RPP_QUERY_FIELDS (shared _fetch_rpp_row) but NOT used by this tool — adjusted_salary is derived per-request from the user-supplied salary.
